title: Create sustainability packet from shipment and warehouse evidence
given: A demo operator has Control Tower, WMS, supplier, and CRM view access.
The tenant has shipments with transport modes, carriers, weight or volume, and Control Tower legs.
WMS billing events are present for warehouse activity.
when: The operator selects Create ESG packet.
then: The assistant persists an AssistantSustainabilityPacket row.
The packet includes shipment emissions, warehouse activity estimate, assumptions, and leadership summary.
No shipment route, carrier, warehouse activity, supplier, CRM account, or external ESG claim is changed automatically.

title: Queue greener execution and data-gap review
given: A sustainability packet exists with an air shipment recommendation and missing ESG data gaps.
The operator has edit access for the sustainability workspace evidence.
when: The operator adds an approval note.
then: An AssistantActionQueueItem is created for sustainability review.
The packet status changes to REVIEW_QUEUED and stores the approval note.
The audit event says routing, carrier, warehouse, supplier, and customer-facing ESG claims were not changed automatically.

title: Permission scoped ESG reporting with explicit assumptions
given: A user lacks either operational evidence access or partner evidence access.
Shipment factors are default planning assumptions rather than audited regulatory factors.
when: A permitted user reviews the preview assumptions.
then: The unpermitted user receives an access denied response.
The permitted user sees default factor assumptions and a do-not-publish guardrail.
Customer or board-ready ESG language remains review-gated until evidence and factors are approved.
